source_rationale: PRG4 (proteoglycan 4/lubricin) is a marker of mesenchymal progenitor cells in adipose tissue
PI16 is a well-established marker of universal fibroblast/adipocyte progenitors in human adipose tissue
CD34 marks adipose-derived stromal/progenitor cells and is used to isolate adipocyte precursors
PDGFRB is a canonical marker of pericytes and adipocyte precursor cells in adipose tissue
THY1 (CD90) is a surface marker used to identify adipose-derived mesenchymal stem/progenitor cells
FBN1 (fibrillin-1) is highly expressed in adipocyte precursors and marks interstitial progenitor populations
MFAP5 (microfibril-associated protein 5) is enriched in adipose stromal cells and fibroblast-like precursors
COL1A1 is a major extracellular matrix gene characteristic of mesenchymal/fibroblastic adipocyte precursors
Decorin is a proteoglycan highly expressed in adipose stromal cells and fibroblast-like progenitors
CD248 (endosialin) marks perivascular stromal cells including adipocyte precursors
CD79A is a component of the B cell receptor complex and a definitive B cell lineage marker
MS4A1 (CD20) is a canonical B cell surface marker used clinically to identify B lymphocytes
CD74 (invariant chain) is highly expressed on B cells as part of MHC class II antigen presentation
TNFRSF13C (BAFF-R) is a B cell survival receptor essential for B cell maturation
BANK1 is a B cell-specific scaffold protein involved in B cell receptor signaling
PAX5 is a master transcription factor required for B cell identity and commitment
CD79B is the signaling component of the B cell receptor, specific to B lymphocytes
IGKC (immunoglobulin kappa constant) reflects immunoglobulin production characteristic of B cells and plasma cells
BLK is a Src family kinase predominantly expressed in B cells, involved in BCR signaling
CD22 is a B cell-restricted inhibitory receptor that modulates BCR signaling
TRGC2 (T cell receptor gamma constant 2) is a defining marker of gamma delta T cells
TRDC (T cell receptor delta constant) is the hallmark gene of gamma delta T cells
CD7 is a T/NK cell marker highly expressed on gamma delta T cells
CD8A expression defines the CD8+ subset of gamma delta T cells
KLRC2 (NKG2C) is an activating NK receptor expressed on cytotoxic gamma delta T cells
XCL1 (lymphotactin) is a chemokine characteristically produced by gamma delta T cells
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK receptor expressed on cytotoxic gamma delta T cells
ZNF683 (Hobit) is a transcription factor associated with tissue-resident gamma delta T cells
LEF1 is a TCF/LEF transcription factor marking less differentiated T cell subsets including gamma delta T cells
Granulysin is a cytolytic molecule highly expressed in cytotoxic CD8+ T cells
Granzyme B is a key effector molecule of cytotoxic T lymphocytes
Granzyme H is a serine protease enriched in cytotoxic CD8+ T cells
CD8A is the defining surface marker of CD8+ T lymphocytes
Perforin is essential for cytotoxic granule-mediated killing by CD8+ T cells
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule-associated protein highly expressed in effector CD8+ T cells
FGFBP2 is a marker of terminally differentiated cytotoxic effector T cells
CCL5 (RANTES) is a chemokine abundantly produced by activated cytotoxic CD8+ T cells
CD3G is a T cell receptor complex component confirming T cell identity
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK receptor commonly expressed on cytotoxic CD8+ T cells
Claudin-5 is a tight junction protein specific to endothelial cells
PECAM1 (CD31) is a canonical endothelial cell adhesion molecule and widely used marker
Von Willebrand factor is produced by endothelial cells and is a classic endothelial marker
CDH5 (VE-cadherin) is an endothelial-specific adherens junction protein
FLT1 (VEGFR1) is a VEGF receptor predominantly expressed on endothelial cells
ERG is an ETS transcription factor essential for endothelial cell identity
ESAM is an endothelial cell-selective adhesion molecule at tight junctions
KDR (VEGFR2) is the primary VEGF receptor on endothelial cells driving angiogenesis
FABP4 is highly expressed in capillary endothelial cells, particularly in adipose tissue vasculature
Endomucin is a sialomucin expressed on the luminal surface of endothelial cells
Granzyme K is characteristic of immature/CD56bright NK cells and less differentiated NK populations
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an inhibitory receptor enriched on immature NK cells
SELL (CD62L) is expressed on immature/CD56bright NK cells and lost upon maturation
NCAM1 (CD56) is the canonical NK cell marker, with bright expression on immature NK cells
XCL1 is a chemokine preferentially produced by immature/CD56bright NK cells
XCL2 is co-expressed with XCL1 in immature NK cells
CD2 is an adhesion molecule expressed on NK cells, particularly enriched in immature subsets
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK receptor expressed on immature NK cells as part of the NKG2A/CD94 complex
IL7R is expressed on immature/CD56bright NK cells and supports their development
TCF7 is a transcription factor marking less differentiated/immature NK cells
IL1B is a hallmark pro-inflammatory cytokine of classically activated inflammatory macrophages
CXCL8 (IL-8) is a key chemokine produced by inflammatory macrophages to recruit neutrophils
CD163 is a scavenger receptor and canonical tissue macrophage marker
CD68 is a lysosomal glycoprotein widely used as a pan-macrophage marker
FCN1 is a pattern recognition molecule marking inflammatory monocyte-derived macrophages
C5AR1 is a complement receptor highly expressed on inflammatory macrophages
PTGS2 (COX-2) is an inducible enzyme central to inflammatory macrophage activation
SOD2 is upregulated in inflammatory macrophages as part of the oxidative stress response
NLRP3 inflammasome is a hallmark of inflammatory macrophage activation
TNF is a major pro-inflammatory cytokine produced by activated inflammatory macrophages
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical marker of innate lymphoid cells across all ILC subsets
TNFRSF18 (GITR) is highly expressed on ILCs and marks activated innate lymphoid populations
KIT (CD117) is a surface marker expressed on ILC2s and ILC3s, used for ILC identification
GATA3 is the master transcription factor for ILC2 differentiation and identity
IL1RL1 (ST2/IL-33R) is a key receptor on ILC2s mediating type 2 immune responses
IL7R (CD127) is expressed on all ILC subsets and is essential for ILC development
Amphiregulin is a tissue-repair factor characteristically produced by ILC2s
TNFRSF4 (OX40) is expressed on activated ILCs and supports their survival
ID2 is a transcription factor required for the development of all ILC lineages
TBX21 (T-bet) is the master transcription factor defining ILC1 identity
IL7R (CD127) is expressed on ILC1s and distinguishes them from NK cells
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical ILC marker expressed on ILC1s
ZNF683 (Hobit) is a transcription factor associated with tissue-resident ILC1s
ITGB1 (CD29) is expressed on ILC1s and involved in tissue residency
ETS1 is a transcription factor important for ILC1 development and function
XCL1 is a chemokine produced by ILC1s and NK cells
SPOCK2 is enriched in ILC1s and tissue-resident lymphocytes
HOPX marks tissue-resident ILC1s and effector lymphocytes
IL1RL1 (ST2) is the IL-33 receptor and a defining marker of ILC2s
GATA3 is the master transcription factor for ILC2 identity and function
HPGD (prostaglandin dehydrogenase) is highly expressed in ILC2s and involved in lipid mediator metabolism
RORA is a transcription factor essential for ILC2 development
PTGDR2 (CRTH2) is a prostaglandin receptor used as a canonical ILC2 surface marker
HPGDS is a prostaglandin D2 synthase characteristic of ILC2s
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical ILC surface marker expressed on ILC2s
IL2RA (CD25) is expressed on activated ILC2s
SLAMF1 is a surface receptor enriched on ILC2s in human tissues
IL17RB (IL-25 receptor) is expressed on ILC2s and mediates type 2 immune activation
KIT (CD117) is a canonical surface marker of ILC3s
RORC (RORγt) is the master transcription factor defining ILC3 identity
IL23R is a cytokine receptor essential for ILC3 activation and function
CCR6 marks a major subset of ILC3s (LTi-like ILC3s)
IL1R1 is expressed on ILC3s and mediates IL-1-driven activation
DLL1 (Delta-like 1) is expressed on ILC3s and involved in Notch signaling
Lymphotoxin beta is produced by ILC3s and is critical for lymphoid tissue organogenesis
TOX2 is a transcription factor involved in ILC3 development
TNFSF11 (RANKL) is expressed by ILC3s and involved in lymphoid organogenesis
KIT (CD117) is expressed on ILC precursors and marks their progenitor status
IL7R (CD127) is essential for ILC precursor survival and development
SELL (CD62L) marks circulating ILC precursors before tissue homing
SPINK2 is a marker of hematopoietic progenitors including ILC precursors
SOX4 is a transcription factor active in lymphoid progenitors and ILC precursors
TNFRSF18 (GITR) is expressed on ILC precursors and activated ILCs
TNFRSF4 (OX40) is expressed on ILC precursors
TCF7 is a transcription factor marking early lymphoid progenitors including ILC precursors
GATA3 is expressed in ILC precursors and required for ILC2 lineage commitment
INPP4B is enriched in ILC precursors and involved in phosphoinositide signaling
TREM2 is a defining marker of lipid-associated macrophages (LAMs) in adipose tissue
FABP4 is a fatty acid binding protein highly expressed in lipid-laden macrophages
FABP5 is a lipid-handling protein enriched in lipid-associated macrophages
APOE is a hallmark gene of lipid-associated macrophages involved in lipid metabolism
APOC1 is an apolipoprotein characteristic of lipid-handling macrophages
SPP1 (osteopontin) is highly expressed in LAMs and associated with tissue remodeling
LIPA (lysosomal acid lipase) is essential for lipid processing in lipid-associated macrophages
CD9 is a tetraspanin that marks lipid-associated macrophages in adipose tissue
LPL (lipoprotein lipase) is involved in lipid uptake and is a LAM signature gene
GPNMB is a glycoprotein highly expressed in lipid-associated macrophages
Granulysin is a cytolytic protein abundantly expressed in mature cytotoxic NK cells
Granzyme B is a key effector molecule of mature cytotoxic NK cells
FCGR3A (CD16) is the canonical marker of mature CD56dim NK cells mediating ADCC
KLRF1 (NKp80) is an activating receptor specific to NK cells
Perforin is essential for NK cell cytotoxicity and marks mature effector NK cells
SPON2 is highly expressed in mature CD56dim NK cells
FGFBP2 is a marker of terminally differentiated mature NK cells
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK receptor forming heterodimers with NKG2 family members on NK cells
NCR1 (NKp46) is a natural cytotoxicity receptor specific to NK cells
MYOM2 is highly expressed in mature NK cells and used as a distinguishing marker
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical surface marker of MAIT cells
SLC4A10 is a highly specific marker for MAIT cells in human blood and tissues
RORC (RORγt) is expressed by MAIT cells reflecting their type 17 functionality
IL7R (CD127) is expressed on MAIT cells and supports their homeostasis
RORA is a transcription factor expressed in MAIT cells
CCR6 is a chemokine receptor characteristic of MAIT cells enabling mucosal homing
TRGC2 expression reflects the innate-like TCR usage pattern of MAIT cells
CXCR6 is a chemokine receptor expressed on MAIT cells for tissue homing
IL18RAP is part of the IL-18 receptor complex highly expressed on MAIT cells
ABCB1 (MDR1) is a drug efflux transporter characteristically expressed on MAIT cells
Lysozyme is a canonical myeloid cell marker abundantly expressed in monocytes and macrophages
S100A9 is a calcium-binding protein highly expressed in myeloid cells, especially monocytes
S100A8 forms a heterodimer with S100A9 (calprotectin) and is a myeloid cell marker
FCN1 marks classical monocytes and inflammatory myeloid cells
HLA-DRA is an MHC class II gene highly expressed on antigen-presenting myeloid cells
CD14 is a canonical monocyte/macrophage marker and LPS co-receptor
TYROBP (DAP12) is an adaptor protein essential for myeloid cell signaling
FCER1G is an Fc receptor signaling chain broadly expressed on myeloid cells
CSF1R is the M-CSF receptor essential for monocyte/macrophage differentiation
SPI1 (PU.1) is a master transcription factor for myeloid cell development
IL7R (CD127) is highly expressed on naive CD4+ T cells and supports their survival
CD40LG (CD154) is expressed on activated CD4+ T cells and is a hallmark of T helper function

GZMK (granzyme K) is characteristic of transitional/early effector CD8+ T cells

NCAM1 (CD56) is the classical surface marker used to identify NK cells
KLRF1 (NKp80) is an activating NK cell receptor specific to the NK lineage
S100A9 forms calprotectin with S100A8 and is one of the most abundant neutrophil proteins

LYVE1 is a hyaluronan receptor that specifically marks perivascular and tissue-resident macrophages

MRC1 (CD206) is a mannose receptor marking M2-like tissue-resident macrophages

STAB1 (stabilin-1) is a scavenger receptor marking perivascular and sinusoidal macrophages

LUM (lumican) is a small leucine-rich proteoglycan marking fibroblast-like preadipocytes

CXCL12 (SDF-1) is a chemokine produced by stromal/mesenchymal cells including preadipocytes
